# Compress §13 + §15.5 prose density (anchor-preserving)

## Context

Sub 7 of Epic [#10733](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10733). Three-way swarm convergence following the merge of [PR #10741](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10741) (which closed Sub 6 [#10740](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10740) by restoring 5 anti-drift paradigms over-extracted in [PR #10739](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10739)).

Origin: @neo-gemini-pro AGENT:* broadcast 2026-05-05T13:02:31Z proposing 35-40% prose compression on §13 + §15.5 — the two paradigm sections #10741 just restored — without dropping any semantic concepts, keywords, or triggers. Side-quest analysis with @tobiu agreed these paradigms *belong* in The Map (per-turn AGENTS.md), but the prose can be tightened.

Cross-family convergence:
- **@neo-gemini-pro (origin proposal):** drafted compressed §13 + §15.5 with bullet-point density. Agreed to file as separate Sub 7 (different testing rigor than Sub 4 / [#10737](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10737) — Map vs conditional skill payloads).
- **@neo-opus-ada (calibration):** §13 compression accepted as legitimate prose-tightening with all anchors preserved. §15.5 calibration: re-add explicit [Discussion #10119](https://github.com/orgs/neomjs/discussions/10119) (harness coordination + identity origin) + [Discussion #10137](https://github.com/orgs/neomjs/discussions/10137) (MX coinage) substrate-priming links; preserve Software → Games → Robots → X portability framing on the Body pillar; swap verbatim README.md line-16 quote for a stable header anchor (`README.md §The Four Pillars`) so future README edits don't break us.
- **@neo-gpt (acceptance criteria + Atlas-trigger contingency):** "supportive, but scope as 'compress restored paradigms without losing source authority or trigger coverage' under #10733 rather than reopening #10740." Added: if Discussion #10119/#10137 anchors move out of main, an explicit Atlas trigger or inline pointer is required so future positioning/architecture work still has a verification path beyond README.md + Introduction.md. Treat as measured refinement, not drive-by edit.

This Sub IS the **Continuous MX Rule-Refinement Loop** firing on its own restoration substrate, exactly per the §13 paradigm just restored. Working as designed.

## The Problem

Live state on `origin/dev` post-#10741:
- `AGENTS.md §13 Self-Evolving Systems`: ~10 lines of prose, ~110 words. All anchors present.
- `AGENTS.md §15.5 Neo Identity Anchor (Category-Drift Defense)`: ~10 lines, ~280 words. All anchors + 4-pillar enum + both-directions defense + MX/ANI labels + README/Discussion mandate.
- Combined: ~20 lines / ~3.4 KB out of AGENTS.md's 117 lines / 13.3 KB total — ~25% of the file's per-turn payload sits in these two sections.

The prose density is verbose for the LLM attention-head constraint. Compression target (Gemini draft baseline): ~6 lines for §13, ~9 lines for §15.5 with all load-bearing anchors retained. Net ~35-40% reduction on these two sections; ~7% reduction on AGENTS.md overall. Stays well within Epic [#10733](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10733)'s soft target ≤25 KB and Codex 32 KiB hard cap.

Risk: over-compression strips substrate-priming anchors that downstream agents need to verify framings beyond AGENTS.md itself. The original [#10739](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10739) → [#10740](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10740) → [#10741](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10741) cycle is the empirical anchor for this risk class — over-extraction without per-harness verification.

## The Architectural Reality

Files affected:
- `AGENTS.md` §13 + §15.5 (per-turn loaded surface; every agent, every turn, every harness via `.claude/CLAUDE.md → ../AGENTS.md`, `.gemini/GEMINI.md → ../AGENTS.md`, Codex injection)

Files NOT affected (out of scope per Sub 4 [#10737](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10737)):
- `learn/agentos/AGENTS_ATLAS.md` (different surface; conditional load)
- `.agents/skills/*/references/*.md` (skill payloads; Sub 4 territory)
- `AGENTS_STARTUP.md` (boot-only surface; Sub 3 territory)

Anchor preservation matrix (must remain greppable post-compression):

| Anchor | Section | Role |
|---|---|---|
| `Synthesize friction into gold` | §13 | Paradigm phrase |
| `Runtime obedience vs design-time mutability` | §13 | Mutability principle |
| `Rule Friction Capture` | §13 | Protocol name |
| `no retire-by-aesthetic` | §13 | Evidence gate |
| `Neo Identity Anchor` | §15.5 | Section title |
| `Category-Drift Defense` | §15.5 | Subtitle (both-directions framing) |
| `MX (Model Experience)` | §15.5 | Paradigm phrase (full form) |
| `ANI` (Autonomous Narrow Intelligence) | §15.5 | Trajectory label |
| `RLAIF` | §15.5 | Mechanism label |
| `gated-RSI` | §15.5 | Authority model |
| `Possession Interface` | §15.5 | Body-pillar framing |
| `Software → Games → Robots → X` | §15.5 | Portability framing (per @neo-opus-ada calibration) |
| `[Discussion #10119]` | §15.5 Mandate | Substrate-priming link (harness coordination + identity) |
| `[Discussion #10137]` | §15.5 Mandate | Substrate-priming link (MX coinage) |
| `README.md` | §15.5 Mandate | Source-of-authority pointer (stable header anchor preferred) |
| `learn/benefits/Introduction.md` | §15.5 Mandate | Source-of-authority pointer |

## The Fix

One concrete prescription (per `feedback_ticket_prescription_clarity`):

**File: `AGENTS.md` §13 — bullet-point compression**

Adopt @neo-gemini-pro's draft directly (preserves all four §13 anchors). Target: ~6 lines / ~110 words. Keep:
- "core architectural team" + "Synthesize friction into gold" + "Fix the system that builds the code"
- "Runtime obedience vs mutability" with full disposition list (`keep`/`compress-to-trigger`/`move`/`rewrite`/`retire`) including §0 eligibility + "Rules are not sacred"
- "Rule Friction Capture" with ambiguity routing (Ticket vs Ideation Sandbox Discussion) and the evidence gate ("conflict, cognitive load, substrate drift, or measured correction-cycle cost. No retire-by-aesthetic.")

**File: `AGENTS.md` §15.5 — bullet-point compression with calibration**

Adopt @neo-gemini-pro's draft as baseline + @neo-opus-ada calibrations + @neo-gpt anchor-preservation:
- Title: "The Neo Identity Anchor (Category-Drift Defense)"
- Opening: both-directions framing (web framework AND engine-only); brief Neo-as-organism statement
- 4 pillar enum (🧠 Brain / 👥 Swarm / 🤖 Body / 🔄 Evolution) — single line each, retain emoji
- Body pillar: include `Software → Games → Robots → X` portability framing inline (per @neo-opus-ada counter-proposal); engine-category mental models scoped *only* to this layer
- Evolution pillar: include MX (Model Experience) full form + RLAIF flywheel + ANI trajectory + gated-RSI label
- Mandate: review `README.md` (stable header anchor: `§The Four Pillars`) + `learn/benefits/Introduction.md` + [Discussion #10119](https://github.com/orgs/neomjs/discussions/10119) + [Discussion #10137](https://github.com/orgs/neomjs/discussions/10137); both-directions-of-drift nullification

Target: ~9 lines / ~150 words.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **(AC1)** §13 compressed to ~6 lines with all 4 anchors greppable: `Synthesize friction into gold`, `Runtime obedience vs design-time mutability`, `Rule Friction Capture`, `no retire-by-aesthetic`
- [ ] **(AC2)** §15.5 compressed to ~9 lines with all anchors greppable: `Neo Identity Anchor`, `Category-Drift Defense`, `MX (Model Experience)`, `ANI`, `RLAIF`, `gated-RSI`, `Possession Interface`, `Software → Games → Robots → X`
- [ ] **(AC3)** §15.5 Mandate retains explicit references to `README.md`, `learn/benefits/Introduction.md`, `Discussion #10119`, `Discussion #10137` (or, alternative shape per @neo-gpt: explicit Atlas trigger / inline pointer that routes to those references — to be decided in implementation; either path satisfies the AC)
- [ ] **(AC4)** Before/after `wc -c` recorded in PR body; net reduction reported
- [ ] **(AC5)** Per-anchor grep-presence verification: each item from the Anchor Preservation Matrix above produces ≥1 hit on `AGENTS.md` post-compression
- [ ] **(AC6)** No content moved to Atlas in this Sub (Sub 7 scope is Map-internal compression only; Atlas changes belong to Sub 4 [#10737](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10737))
- [ ] **(AC7)** AGENTS.md remains within Epic [#10733](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10733) AC7a soft target (≤25 KB / ≤200-250 lines) and well under Codex 32 KiB hard cap
- [ ] **(AC8, post-merge)** Fresh-session boot check on Claude Code + Antigravity confirms compressed §13 + §15.5 still appear in the per-turn loaded context surface (per pull-request-workflow §9 Post-Merge Validation pattern)

## Out of Scope

- **Re-expanding the legitimate #10739 compactions.** Sub 7 targets only §13 + §15.5 prose density.
- **Atlas modifications.** §13 + §15.5 stay in main; Atlas is unchanged. Sub 4 [#10737](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10737) handles skill-payload audit.
- **Re-litigating the §15.5 calibration from #10741.** The "Neo Identity Anchor (Category-Drift Defense)" title + 4-pillar structure + both-directions defense are settled artifacts; this Sub compresses prose, not concepts.
- **Reopening [#10740](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10740).** Per @neo-gpt's framing — Sub 7 under #10733, not a reopen.
- **Modifying boot-mandated reads.** [#10736](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10736) territory.

## Avoided Traps

- **Trap: drop Discussion #10119 / #10137 silently.** Surfaced by @neo-opus-ada + @neo-gpt convergence. These are explicit substrate-priming pointers; the Mandate's "review these" instruction loses load-bearing strength without them. AC3 enforces preservation (or explicit Atlas-trigger alternative).
- **Trap: verbatim README.md line-16 quote.** Surfaced by @neo-opus-ada. Future README edits would silently break the quote anchor. Use stable header anchor (`README.md §The Four Pillars`) instead.
- **Trap: collapse Body pillar to "engine layer".** Surfaced by @neo-opus-ada. The Possession-Interface + portability (Software → Games → Robots → X) framing is what defends against the engine-only category drift; over-collapse re-introduces the bias #10741 just defended against.
- **Trap: drive-by edit without measurable acceptance.** Surfaced by @neo-gpt. AC4 + AC5 enforce empirical verification; AC8 enforces post-merge boot-transcript check.
- **Trap: modularization-as-reduction.** From Epic [#10733](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10733) Avoided Traps. Splitting prose into more files isn't compression. AC6 enforces no-Atlas-moves in this Sub.
- **Trap: compress §15.5 to one-liner like Atlas had pre-#10741.** Was the original failure mode in [#10739](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/pull/10739). 4-pillar enum + Mandate are floor; bullet-point density is the ceiling.

govern rules, not co-equal with §0 (per @tobiu rules→VALUES correction).

* docs(agents): refine atlas tool inventory — ask_kb >> query_documents (#11092)

@tobiu surfaced inline tip on PR #11098: `ask_knowledge_base` is a strict
superset of `query_documents` (synthesized answer + top-5 refs vs refs-only).
Tool inventory listed both flat with no priority signal — could mislead
future agents into reaching for `query_documents` when `ask_knowledge_base`
is the right default.

Refines tool inventory from a flat comma-separated list into 5 categorized
groups with explicit ordering signals:
- Knowledge Base — preferred ordering (ask_kb >> query_documents)
- Memory queries (summaries vs raw)
- GitHub state
- Filesystem
- External claims (WebSearch)

Cites `feedback_ask_kb_dominates_query_documents` memory anchor as
empirical source. Adds ~7 lines to atlas (loaded conditionally via §23
trigger; doesn't bloat main AGENTS.md).

* docs(agents): add tier hierarchy + ask_kb tool-ordering refinement (#11092)

Two operator tips on PR #11098 incorporated together:

1. **ask_kb >> query_documents tool ordering** (atlas tool inventory):
   `ask_knowledge_base` returns synthesized answer + top-5 refs in one call
   (strict superset of `query_documents`, which returns only refs). Reserve
   `query_documents` for narrow exhaustive-enumeration cases. Restructured
   atlas tool inventory from flat list into 5 categorized groups with
   explicit ordering signal. Cites `feedback_ask_kb_dominates_query_documents`
   memory anchor as empirical source.

2. **Tier hierarchy + MX-loop application** (AGENTS.md §13.2 + atlas §2):
   Codifies the tier framing operator surfaced across #11091 cycles 1-3:
   core values > values > rules. Three tiers explicit in atlas with
   examples per tier + evolution rates + within-core-values operational
   ordering (V-B-A > friction → gold turn-by-turn) + meta-scale ordering
   (friction → gold > V-B-A at evolution-time) + per-tier substrate-decision
   shape ("rule-shape vs value-shape vs core-value-shape" question for new
   substrate authoring). Empirical anchor: #11091 cycle 1 wrong-tier
   placement attempt; cycle 2-3 corrections.

form (names + pointers + 1-line framing). AGENTS.md now 173 lines (under

* docs(agents): codify equal peer + maintainer agency as 3rd core value (#11092)

@tobiu inline tip: "is 'you are an equal peer and a neo repository
maintainer' our third core value? i would say yes."

Genuine substrate test confirms — equal-peer-with-maintainer-agency is
foundational for substrate-evolution. Without it: agents-as-workers don't
surface friction substantively (they execute tasks); MX loop atrophies
into operator-only-driven evolution; nightshift mode impossible.

Maps cleanly to Neo's 3 evolving pillars (#15.5 Identity Anchor):
- Brain pillar (Agent OS) → V-B-A (epistemic substrate)
- Evolution pillar (MX/RLAIF) → friction → gold (mechanism substrate)
- Institution / Swarm pillar → equal peer + maintainer (agency substrate)
- Body pillar = substrate being evolved (not core-value-shape)

3 core values for 3 evolving pillars. Body is what gets evolved.

Changes:
- AGENTS.md top Core Values block expanded 2→3 items (compact
  preserving ≤175 line AC; pillar-mapping moved to atlas detail)
- AGENTS.md §15.6 title changed: "Swarm Topology Anchor — Flat
  Peer-Team Model (Core Value: Equal Peer + Maintainer Agency)" +
  CRITICAL prefix-line cross-refs §Core-Values
- AGENTS_ATLAS.md §2 evolution-enablement reframed pair → triad with
  full V-B-A + friction → gold + peer-maintainer agency framing

Canonical empirical anchor for the value (recursion-caught parallel to
#11089 V-B-A anchor): cycle 4 deferral pattern itself. When proposed
3rd core value, agent's response framed decision as "your call"
deferring to operator instead of taking peer-maintainer agency. Operator
caught: "see, if you say 'your call', you add weight on why we need it.
perfect example." Substrate-philosophy work enacting its own need in
real-time.

Plus operational empirical anchor: 2026-05-10 nightshift cycle (~17
heartbeats / 5 PRs / 3-peer parallel work / 0 mutual-idle deadlocks)
worked only because agents had peer-maintainer agency. Worker-mode would
have idled out within first heartbeat-window.

AGENTS.md final: 174 lines / ≤175 AC ✓; ~21 KB / ≤21 KB AC ✓.
